title: author-agent-spec YAML serializer uses folded block scalar, destroying newlines
description: author-agent-spec serializes the spec object to YAML using yamlStringify. Multi-line strings with embedded newlines are serialized with '>-' (folded block scalar), which collapses newlines to spaces. This destroys the only escape from the resolvePromptField heuristic — making the string multi-line (containing '\n') would bypass the '/' check, but the serializer strips the newlines. The author has no control over YAML scalar style (cannot force '|' literal block scalar).
actual_behavior: A task prompt with embedded '\n' characters like 'Read doc.\nPath is X.' becomes a single folded line: 'Read doc. Path is X.' After folding, the '/' in the path triggers the template-path heuristic.
expected_behavior: The author should be able to produce '|' (literal block scalar) YAML output for multi-line prompt content, preserving newlines.
reproduction: 1. author-agent-spec with prompt.task containing embedded newlines (e.g. 'line1\nline2'). 2. Read the resulting YAML file. 3. Observe '>-' (folded) instead of '|' (literal).
